title: MANGA NGE Individual scenes
wikitext:
[]: [NGE] Individual scenes             Quote Post by Olin of Xephon » Tue Aug 03, 2004 9:46 pm Alright so some people like the anime more then the manga and visa versa. But were do you think the manga beats the anime? Which scene(s) do you think were handled better? And which scenes does the anime do better then the manga? Taking it a step further, which Shinji, Rei, Asuka, etc. etc. do you like better, Anime or Manga? Personally, I liked it better when Shinji woke up in the hospital after the dream with the Eva, got piked up by Misato and then broke down after seeing the city. It just worked better for me. Another scene I think Yoshyuki nailed would be Shinji's rescue of Ayanami fro the entry plug after their fight with ramiel (the big cube) As far as the anime goes, I'm sorry, but few things match Shinji's first encounter with Pen Pen. And having Shinji talk with Kaji out in the garden overlooked by a decapitated unit-02 was better. (although that way we don't hear about Kaji's childhood) Character wise, I like Sadamoto's Rei better. And while I love manga Shinji, Annos' seems a more honest representation of the character.

[]: Re: Individual scenes             Quote Post by houtaru » Tue Aug 03, 2004 11:42 pm Olin of Xephon wrote: Character wise, I like Sadamoto's Rei better. And while I love manga Shinji, Annos' seems a more honest representation of the character.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Aug 04, 2004 12:55 am The good thing about the manga is that Sadamoto has had lots of time to think about how to tell the story. Things about the manga I liked better: The way in which Shinji still feels awkward around Rei due to the incident in her apartment. Sadamoto really conveyed the "wrongness" of that scene so much better. The expanded scene after the defeat of Ramiel. The differences in volume 6, such as Touji's fear of piloting, and Shinji's knowledge of Touji's piloting, and as well, Touji dying. It was just a real heart wrenching series of events. Kaji's backstory. Less filler. Things about the manga I didn't like: No melon patch scene. No leliel. Kaworu is a fucking cat killer.

[]:             Quote Post by EVA-01X » Wed Aug 04, 2004 11:34 am Things I liked The nice little WAFFy Shinji/Asuka bit in v.5 Shinji has a sembelance of a backbone most of the time Unit-01 is sooooo much more frightning in the manga when it goes berserk in v.7 They show just how they got Shogouki back into the hanger after that little incident... Things I didn't Like Karwou, 'nuff said. They killed Touji! (Those basterds...) A little too Rei-Centric... I like Asuka myself.

[]:             Quote Post by the-artist-known-as-chris » Wed Aug 04, 2004 6:38 pm I like the Manga better, for many reasons. One being certain chracters like Kaji. He is handeld SO MUCH better in the manga, his past, his diouloge, eveything is better. I liked how Shinji knew that Toji was the pilot, it made the whole scene of his eva tearing apart toji's much more dramatic. And while it's sad that Toji dies in the manga, overall it was a better end to his character arc since Toji pretty much does nothing else in the anime after that. The hug Shinji and Misato shared in volume 2 was touching aswell.

[]:             Quote Post by max3000 » Wed Aug 04, 2004 7:34 pm stuff i liked Rei inviting Shinji to her apartment the day after the "incident". Kaworu killing a cat. <-- puts more personality into him imo ..And i hate cats.. DIE HELLO KITTY MUAHAHHAHA Shinji finaly breaks out and punches Gendo. But Kaji stopped him. And also like Aaron said: Kaji's back story and the extended scene after Ramiel. Over all, i like the way it was stream-lined to make the plot flow better. Oh and ofcourse Sadomoto-sensei's artwork stuff i didn't like Not much to deslike imo :P

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Wed Aug 04, 2004 8:11 pm As far as characters go, I look at it like this: The anime created the characters, and the manga fleshed them out, showing sides of them we wouldn't normally have a chance to see (I know, the manga came out first, but... still).

[]:             Quote Post by KaworuNagisa17 » Wed Aug 04, 2004 10:48 pm Kaworu killed a cat? ;-;

[]: Fleshing it out...             Quote Post by Olin of Xephon » Wed Aug 04, 2004 11:05 pm I agree, they handled Kaji a lot better. And I still can't comment on the whole Kaouru killed a cat thing since I'm waiting for the english release, but I think I'll probably like the manga version of him better! :) I also think the manga seems to take some of the anime's "mistakes" and fix them. Shinji's clothes in the plug rather then the plug suit, how they subdued eva-01, etc. The manga seems to flesh out whatever the anime missed. As I've always said, they compliment eachother.

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Wed Aug 04, 2004 11:12 pm I don't like the fact that Kaji smokes all the time :x i found it anoying

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Fri Aug 06, 2004 1:40 pm I like the manga a lot. I like it so much that It's getting hard for me to decide which I like better! I'm a huge Rei fan, so that gives me more incentive to like the manga. I'm also a big Kaworu fan, so I like the fact he is introduced earlier in the manga. :shock: But killing the poor neko :cry: :cry: I love manga, but, I like the anime...cuz I don't have to read it I love to read, but the manga doesn't really do the EVA's motion justice. I think I'm just rambling here. Gomen :oops:

[]:             Quote Post by RubberSoul » Sat Aug 07, 2004 3:29 am What I like: You get to see Eva a bit more from Shinji's POV You get to see more of Kaworu Sadamoto's art The characters seem a bit less isolated from each other. Shinji almost punched Gendo Kaworu seems to have more of a personality What I don't like: It's not finished yet. Toji's death Some parts of it are a little cheesy (EX: Asuka beating those kids up) Lack of interaction between Asuka and Shinji

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sun Aug 08, 2004 6:59 am RubberSoul wrote: What I like: You get to see Eva a bit more from Shinji's POV You get to see more of Kaworu Sadamoto's art The characters seem a bit less isolated from each other. Shinji almost punched Gendo Kaworu seems to have more of a personality What I don't like: It's not finished yet. Toji's death Some parts of it are a little cheesy (EX: Asuka beating those kids up) Lack of interaction between Asuka and Shinji

[]: You thought that was cheesy!?             Quote Post by Olin of Xephon » Sun Aug 08, 2004 11:35 pm Some parts of it are a little cheesy (EX: Asuka beating those kids up)
